entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A prominent pulse crop and protein source in tropical and subtropical regions.

entities.name: TROPICAL AND SUBTROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Regions where pigeonpea is traditionally cultivated.

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Environmental and biological stressors that limit pigeonpea production.

entities.name: MARGINAL LANDS
entities.description: Lands with low productivity where pigeonpea is often cultivated.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The current era of shifting weather patterns and environmental instability.

entities.name: WEATHER CALAMITIES
entities.description: Unforeseen extreme weather events resulting from climate change.

entities.name: PESTS AND DISEASES
entities.description: Biological agents that cause significant yield losses in pigeonpea.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: Traditional crop improvement approaches used to develop high-yielding cultivars.

entities.name: GENETIC BASE
entities.description: The range of genetic diversity available within a crop species.

entities.name: DNA SEQUENCING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Advanced tools used to generate large quantities of genomic data.

entities.name: GENOMIC DATA
entities.description: Information regarding the genetic makeup of a crop available in the public domain.

entities.name: GENOMIC-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding approach that integrates genomic data to accelerate genetic gain.

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING PLATFORMS
entities.description: Technologies used to rapidly assess crop traits.

entities.name: GENETIC GAIN
entities.description: The rate of improvement in a trait over successive breeding generations.

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION STUDIES
entities.description: A method used to link genetic markers to specific traits for stress resilience.

entities.name: GENOME EDITING
entities.description: A technology used to precisely modify the genome to improve stress resilience.

entities.name: STRESS RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to maintain productivity under biotic and abiotic stress.
